summary: Reviewed AmbientEditorialHero.tsx, AnatomyFocalCard.tsx, globals.css (hero-shell-poster block at ~5447), frontpage concepts 37-46, and logo options 201-249. Key findings: (1) all 10 frontpage concepts share identical nav/bg/button/stats structure — differentiation is purely in headline copy and anatomy figure geometry; (2) logos 201-249 have a significant duplication problem — options 210, 215, 220, 230, 235, 240, 245 are byte-identical SVGs; (3) AnatomyFocalCard.tsx references hero-focal-* CSS classes extensively but those classes have zero matches in globals.css — the right-side card is rendering as unstyled SVG paths, no premium tone at all.
recommendations: Add hero-focal-* CSS block to globals.css: AnatomyFocalCard references .hero-focal-shell-line, .hero-focal-figure, .hero-focal-spine, .hero-focal-organ, .hero-focal-rib, .hero-focal-heart, .hero-focal-vessel, .hero-focal-detail, .hero-focal-figure-ghost, .hero-focal-axis, .hero-focal-latitude, .hero-focal-latitude-soft, .hero-focal-shell-soft, .hero-focal-shell-ghost, .hero-focal-badge — none have CSS rules. Until this block exists the card renders as raw black strokes. Add a .hero-focal-* section after line 5555 using the established palette: figure stroke rgba(52,47,42,0.28) sw 1.4, organ rgba(193,154,86,0.32) sw 1.6, spine rgba(112,102,89,0.14) sw 1.1, ghost opacity 0.28, badge parchment background.

Strengthen hero-shell-poster anatomy body stroke weight: Current .hero-shell-poster .hero-anatomy-body is rgba(52,47,42,0.21) at stroke-width:0.84 — too faint relative to the concept-37 target which uses rgba(49,42,35,0.30) at sw 2.15. Bump to opacity 0.30 and stroke-width 1.2 to read as an intentional drawn figure, not a ghost. Also bump .hero-anatomy-organ to rgba(193,154,86,0.36) sw 1.4.

Lock to frontpage-37 as the hero layout baseline: Frontpage-37 is the strongest: large globe (r=432, fills the right viewport half), full bilateral figure (head oval, two arm paths, lower bifurcation), teal accent on vertical spine, gold organ arcs. It pairs 'quieter and stronger' headline with the highest-information anatomy mark. Concepts 43/44 (smaller, simplified figure) undermine the clinical-depth signal. Concepts 38/46 use identical anatomy to 37 but weaker headlines. The current live AmbientEditorialHero already approximates this layout — the only gap is the CSS stroke fix in rec #2.

Cull the logo batch — identify the actual distinct variants before picking top 5: Options 210, 215, 220, 230, 235, 240, 245 are identical SVG files (confirmed via content inspection). The actual distinct types in 201-249 are: (A) ECG-waveform + anatomy circle mark (201), (B) dual-orbital ellipses + vertical axis (202), (C) canonical full-figure head+spine+bilateral arms in 3-color palette (representative: 210), (D) editorial wordmark with globe orbits + Georgia serif type (249). Human should diff options 203-209 and 211-214 to find any stroke-weight or proportion variants before finalizing. Top 5 candidates from confirmed distinct types: 201, 202, 210, 249, plus one from the 203-209 range pending human diff.

Frontpage concept SVG structure: shared nav+bg+stats, differentiation only in headline + anatomy geometry
Logo 201-249 batch has heavy duplication — distinct types confirmed: 201 (ECG), 202 (dual-orbit), 210 (full-figure canonical), 249 (wordmark+globe)

Logo options 210-245 are heavily duplicated; only 4 distinct icon types confirmed in the 201-249 batch

active_context: Current sprint: homepage premium direction + logo cull
CSS file: globals.css, target section ~5447 (hero-shell-poster block)
Logo working set: 201, 202, 210, 249 + pending human diff of 203-209

current_blocker: hero-focal-* CSS classes are entirely absent from globals.css. AnatomyFocalCard.tsx renders with no stroke styling — the right-side anatomy field looks like raw SVG, not premium editorial. This must be fixed before the component can be evaluated for creator outreach readiness.
suggested_human_fix: Open apps/web/src/styles/globals.css, go to line 5556 (after the .guild-tavern-shell block). Add a new .hero-focal-* CSS section using the same warm parchment token palette already established in the hero-shell-poster block. Minimum viable rule set: .hero-focal-figure { stroke: rgba(52,47,42,0.28); stroke-width:1.4; fill:none; stroke-linecap:round; stroke-linejoin:round; } .hero-focal-organ, .hero-focal-heart { stroke: rgba(193,154,86,0.34); stroke-width:1.6; fill:none; stroke-linecap:round; } .hero-focal-spine { stroke: rgba(112,102,89,0.14); stroke-width:1.1; fill:none; stroke-linecap:round; } .hero-focal-detail, .hero-focal-rib, .hero-focal-vessel { stroke: rgba(105,93,78,0.16); stroke-width:1.0; fill:none; stroke-linecap:round; } .hero-focal-figure-ghost { opacity:0.28; } .hero-focal-shell-line { stroke: rgba(127,117,102,0.18); stroke-width:1.2; fill:none; } .hero-focal-shell-soft { stroke: rgba(127,117,102,0.07); stroke-width:1.0; fill:none; } .hero-focal-axis, .hero-focal-latitude { stroke: rgba(127,117,102,0.052); stroke-width:1.0; fill:none; } .hero-focal-latitude-soft { stroke-opacity:0.55; } .hero-focal-badge { display:inline-block; font-size:0.72rem; font-weight:600; letter-spacing:0.08em; color:rgba(120,108,92,0.88); background:rgba(255,252,247,0.82); border:1px solid rgba(218,206,188,0.5); border-radius:2rem; padding:0.22rem 0.72rem; }
next_task: Write the hero-focal CSS block (see suggested_human_fix above) and bump hero-shell-poster body stroke weight to opacity 0.30 / sw 1.2. Then reload the homepage — if the right-side card now reads as a premium clinical illustration at 1200px, it is ready for a creator outreach screenshot. Estimated time: 30 min.
